clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Provide evidence-based recommendations for hypertension management including team-based care, follow-up protocols, HIT interventions, and diagnostic testing
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Initial hypertension diagnosis, uncontrolled BP management, medication titration, follow-up evaluation
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Care team assignment + follow-up interval + HIT strategy + lab/ECG ordering

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for plan of care recommendations
Determine follow-up interval based on BP control status
Select HIT interventions (telehealth, EHR, patient registries)
Display team member roles and responsibilities

validationRules: If uncontrolled hypertension → team-based care required
If new or intensified therapy → monthly follow-up
If hypertension diagnosed → labs + ECG required

rules: If uncontrolled hypertension → recommend team-based care (COR 1, LOE A)
If uncontrolled hypertension + SDOH barriers → add HBPM + responsive team-based care (COR 1, LOE C-LD)
Apply integrated treatment model: accurate BP measurement + prompt treatment + patient engagement + ongoing HBPM review (COR 1, LOE B-NR)
If new or intensified therapy → schedule monthly follow-up for medication adherence and response (COR 1, LOE B-R)
If uncontrolled hypertension → incorporate HIT (synchronous/asynchronous) for BP control, access, and medication titration (COR 1, LOE B-R)
If undiagnosed or uncontrolled hypertension → use EHR + patient registries for screening and identification (COR 1, LOE B-NR)
If uncontrolled hypertension → telehealth interventions can reduce BP (COR 2a, LOE B-R)
If hypertension diagnosed → order labs (CBC, electrolytes, creatinine, lipid panel, glucose/HbA1c, TSH, urinalysis, UACR) + 12-lead ECG (COR 1, LOE C-EO)

careTeamMembers.description: Assigned team members based on complexity

requiredLabs.description: CBC, electrolytes, creatinine, lipid panel, glucose/HbA1c, TSH, urinalysis, UACR

appDisplay.behavior: Normal routing to care team assignment

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Implement team-based care with assigned roles based on patient complexity
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monthly until BP controlled, then reassess
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: HBPM with structured follow-up and review
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag resistant HTN or secondary causes for specialist referral (nephrology, endocrinology)
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log care plan to EHR, assign team members in patient registry
